seoTitle: How to Reply to a Zendesk Ticket the Right Way
metaDescription: Learn how to reply to a Zendesk ticket using the agent workspace composer, choose between public replies and internal notes, and avoid sending mistakes.
intro: Replying to a Zendesk ticket sounds simple, but mixing up a public reply with an internal note can accidentally expose team discussion to a customer. This guide covers how to use the conversation composer to draft, format, and send responses safely, plus how to set the right ticket status before you submit.

steps.heading: Open the target ticket
steps.body: From your active support views, click into the ticket you want to respond to so the unified conversation panel loads.

steps.heading: Choose your composer mode
steps.body: Use the composer dropdown to pick between a Public reply, visible to the customer, or an internal note, meant for teammate coordination only.

steps.heading: Draft in a safe mode
steps.body: Turn on draft mode while writing so you can polish your response without risking an accidental early send.

steps.heading: Format your response
steps.body: Apply rich text formatting like bullet points and links, and attach any relevant files directly in the composer.

steps.heading: Insert a macro if needed
steps.body: Use saved macros to pull in pre-written text blocks instead of retyping common answers.

steps.heading: Set the ticket status and submit
steps.body: Pick the appropriate status, such as Open, Pending, or Solved, from the dropdown, then click Submit to send the reply.

faq.question: What's the difference between a public reply and an internal note?
faq.answer: A public reply goes to the customer, while an internal note is only visible to your support team and never leaves the workspace.

faq.question: Can I undo a reply after sending it?
faq.answer: Once submitted, a public reply is delivered to the customer and can't be recalled, so it's worth using draft mode to review before sending.

faq.question: Why isn't my macro inserting the right text?
faq.answer: Check that you selected the correct macro from the list, since similarly named macros can contain different pre-saved content.

faq.question: Do I have to change the ticket status every time I reply?
faq.answer: It's best practice to update the status so the ticket lifecycle accurately reflects whether it's still open, pending the customer, or resolved.
